mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales indicate strong consumer spending, which supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows falls below 0.03, or the long-short portfolio based on RSAFS momentum does not generate significant alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer confidence and spending, which depresses corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns negative over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate the central bank is tightening policy to combat inflation, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL have positive 3-month rate of change
failure_condition: When both proxies are rising, the forward 20-day return distribution is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term in a regression adds no explanatory power beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during the late-cycle expansion phase signal an impending economic slowdown, as residential investment leads the business cycle, and equity markets begin to discount lower future earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by more than 5% from its recent peak
failure_condition: In periods where the regime is identified as late-cycle and the trigger activates, the 60-day forward return is not consistently negative (median return > 0 over multiple episodes).

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an abrupt weakening in the manufacturing sector, which typically precedes broader economic contraction and leads to sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Following trigger events, the 5-day forward return does not show a statistically significant negative mean (p > 0.05) or the win rate for negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent deterioration in payroll employment growth and increase in the unemployment rate provide a robust indication of labor market weakness that erodes consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as recession risks rise.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS month-over-month change negative and UNRATE month-over-month change positive for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, the 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the predictive accuracy measured by precision (negative returns when signal active) is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent upward drift in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and corporate profit margins, forcing the central bank to eventually tighten, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the coming month.
failure_condition: A long-short strategy based on CPIAUCSL momentum (level change) shows Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above the 2% threshold, it marks a transition from accommodative to restrictive policy that raises the cost of capital and reduces equity risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate (negative returns) is below 55%.
